The repo_resolution.py file - find `resolve_owner_repo()`, `get_remote_github()`, `is_remote_mode()`, `repo_option` 3. Any real implementation and fake implementation files 4. Give me the method signatures and a brief description of each.
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.

session.tool_result:
1→"""Abstract interface for remote GitHub operations without a local git clone. 2→ 3→This gateway enables GitHub operations (branch creation, file commits, PR management, 4→workflow dispatch) using the GitHub REST API directly, without requiring a local 5→git repository. All methods take explicit owner/repo parameters. 6→""" 7→ 8→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod 9→ 10→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueInfo, IssueNotFound, PRReference 11→ 12→ 13→class RemoteGitHub(ABC): 14→ """GitHub operations that don't require a local git clone. 15→ 16→ Every method takes explicit owner/repo parameters instead of repo_root: Path. 17→ Uses the GitHub REST API via HttpClient. 18→ """ 19→ 20→ @abstractmethod 21→ def get_authenticated_user(self) -> str: 22→ """Get the username of the authenticated GitHub user. 23→ 24→ Returns: 25→ GitHub username string 26→ """ 27→ ... 28→ 29→ @abstractmethod 30→ def get_default_branch_name(self, *, owner: str, repo: str) -> str:
... [307 lines omitted] ...
329→ Tuple of (is_authenticated, username, error_message)

1→"""Real implementation of RemoteGitHub using HttpClient. 2→ 3→All GitHub operations are performed via the REST API through HttpClient, 4→with no local git repository or gh CLI required. 5→""" 6→ 7→import base64 8→import secrets 9→import string 10→from datetime import datetime 11→ 12→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueInfo, IssueNotFound, PRReference 13→from erk_shared.gateway.http.abc import HttpClient, HttpError 14→from erk_shared.gateway.remote_github.abc import RemoteGitHub 15→from erk_shared.gateway.time.abc import Time 16→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 17→ 18→ 19→class RealRemoteGitHub(RemoteGitHub): 20→ """Production implementation using HttpClient for all GitHub API calls.""" 21→ 22→ def __init__(self, *, http_client: HttpClient, time: Time) -> None: 23→ """Create RealRemoteGitHub. 24→ 25→ Args: 26→ http_client: Authenticated HTTP client for GitHub API 27→ time: Time gateway for polling delays 28→ """ 29→ self._http = http_client 30→ self._time = time
... [394 lines omitted] ...
37→ raise RuntimeError("GitHub API returned user without login field") 45→ raise RuntimeError( 56→ raise RuntimeError( 90→ raise RuntimeError( 119→ raise RuntimeError("GitHub API returned PR without number field") 190→ raise RuntimeError( 201→ raise RuntimeError( 231→ except HttpError as e: 355→ except HttpError as e: 356→ return (False, None, f"Authentication failed: {e}")

1→"""Fake implementation of RemoteGitHub for testing. 2→ 3→Constructor-injected responses for reads, mutation tracking lists for write assertions. 4→""" 5→ 6→from dataclasses import dataclass 7→ 8→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueInfo, IssueNotFound, PRReference 9→from erk_shared.gateway.remote_github.abc import RemoteGitHub 10→ 11→ 12→@dataclass(frozen=True) 13→class CreatedRef: 14→ """Recorded create_ref call.""" 15→ 16→ owner: str 17→ repo: str 18→ ref: str 19→ sha: str 20→ 21→ 22→@dataclass(frozen=True) 23→class CreatedFileCommit: 24→ """Recorded create_file_commit call.""" 25→ 26→ owner: str 27→ repo: str 28→ path: str 29→ content: str 30→ message: str
... [370 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Shared --repo infrastructure for PR read commands. 2→ 3→Provides helpers for resolving owner/repo from either a --repo flag 4→or local git context, and constructing RemoteGitHub instances. 5→""" 6→ 7→import click 8→ 9→from erk.cli.ensure import UserFacingCliError 10→from erk.core.context import ErkContext, NoRepoSentinel 11→from erk_shared.gateway.remote_github.abc import RemoteGitHub 12→from erk_shared.gateway.remote_github.real import RealRemoteGitHub 13→ 14→ 15→def resolve_owner_repo( 16→ ctx: ErkContext, 17→ *, 18→ target_repo: str | None, 19→) -> tuple[str, str]: 20→ """Resolve owner/repo from --repo flag or local git context. 21→ 22→ Args: 23→ ctx: ErkContext 24→ target_repo: Optional "owner/repo" string from --repo flag 25→ 26→ Returns: 27→ Tuple of (owner, repo_name) 28→ 29→ Raises: 30→ UserFacingCliError: If owner/repo cannot be determined

session.assistant:
Now let me compile a comprehensive report of all the findings:
## RemoteGitHub Gateway Pattern Analysis
I found all the RemoteGitHub gateway pattern files you requested. Here's a complete analysis:
### 1. ABC Interface - Method Signatures and Descriptions
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/remote_github/abc.py`
All methods are abstract and take explicit `owner` and `repo` parameters. Here are all 17 methods:
#### Authentication & Basic Repository Info - `get_authenticated_user() -> str` — Get the username of the authenticated GitHub user - `check_auth_status() -> tuple[bool, str | None, str | None]` — Check authentication status via REST API, returns (is_authenticated, username, error_message)
#### Default Branch Operations - `get_default_branch_name(*, owner: str, repo: str) -> str` — Get the default branch name (e.g., "main") - `get_default_branch_sha(*, owner: str, repo: str) -> str` — Get the SHA of the default branch HEAD
#### Git Reference & Commit Operations - `create_ref(*, owner: str, repo: str, ref: str, sha: str) -> None` — Create a git reference/branch (e.g., "refs/heads/my-branch") - `create_file_commit(*, owner: str, repo: str, path: str, content: str, message: str, branch: str) -> str` — Create a file commit via GitHub Contents API, returns commit SHA
#### Pull Request Operations - `create_pull_request(*, owner: str, repo: str, head: str, base: str, title: str, body: str, draft: bool) -> int` — Create a pull request, returns PR number - `update_pull_request_body(*, owner: str, repo: str, pr_number: int, body: str) -> None` — Update PR body content - `close_pr(*, owner: str, repo: str, number: int) -> None` — Close a pull request
#### Issue Operations - `add_labels(*, owner: str, repo: str, issue_number: int, labels: tuple[str, ...]) -> None` — Add labels to an issue or PR - `add_issue_comment(*, owner: str, repo: str, issue_number: int, body: str) -> None` — Add a comment to an issue or PR - `get_issue(*, owner: str, repo: str, number: int) -> IssueInfo | IssueNotFound` — Fetch issue by number - `get_issue_comments(*, owner: str, repo: str, number: int) -> list[str]` — Fetch all comment bodies for an issue - `get_prs_referencing_issue(*, owner: str, repo: str, number: int) -> list[PRReference]` — Get PRs that reference an issue via timeline API - `list_issues(*, owner: str, repo: str, labels: tuple[str, ...], state: str, limit: int | None, creator: str | None) -> list[IssueInfo]` — List issues with filtering - `close_issue(*, owner: str, repo: str, number: int) -> None` — Close a GitHub issue
#### Workflow Operations - `dispatch_workflow(*, owner: str, repo: str, workflow: str, ref: str, inputs: dict[str, str]) -> str` — Dispatch a workflow and poll for run ID, returns workflow run ID
---
### 2. Real Implementation
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/remote_github/real.py`
**Class:** `RealRemoteGitHub`
Uses `HttpClient` for all GitHub REST API calls and `Time` gateway for polling delays. Key implementation details:
- All read operations query GitHub REST API directly - `create_file_commit()` base64-encodes content before sending - `dispatch_workflow()` generates a random 6-character distinct ID for correlation, then polls with exponential backoff (1, 2, 4, 8, 8, 8 seconds) - `get_issue_comments()` uses paginated API with per_page=100 - `list_issues()` skips pull requests (GitHub includes them in issues endpoint) - `get_prs_referencing_issue()` parses timeline events to find cross-references, tracks seen PR numbers to avoid duplicates - Helper function `_parse_issue_response()` converts GitHub REST API responses to IssueInfo objects with timestamp parsing - Helper function `_generate_distinct_id()` creates base36 random strings for workflow dispatch
---
### 3. Fake Implementation
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/remote_github/fake.py`
**Class:** `FakeRemoteGitHub`
Constructor-injected fake for testing with mutation tracking. Key characteristics:
**Constructor parameters:** - `authenticated_user: str` — pre-configured username - `default_branch_name: str` — pre-configured branch name - `default_branch_sha: str` — pre-configured SHA - `next_pr_number: int` — PR number to return from create operations - `dispatch_run_id: str` — workflow run ID to return - `issues: dict[int, IssueInfo] | None` — pre-configured issues - `issue_comments: dict[int, list[str]] | None` — pre-configured comments - `pr_references: dict[int, list[PRReference]] | None` — pre-configured PR references
**Mutation tracking properties** (for test assertions): - `created_refs: list[CreatedRef]` — recorded branch creation calls - `created_file_commits: list[CreatedFileCommit]` — recorded file commits - `created_pull_requests: list[CreatedPullRequest]` — recorded PR creations - `updated_pr_bodies: list[UpdatedPullRequestBody]` — recorded PR updates - `added_labels: list[AddedLabels]` — recorded label additions - `dispatched_workflows: list[DispatchedWorkflow]` — recorded workflow dispatches - `added_issue_comments: list[AddedIssueComment]` — recorded issue comments - `closed_issues: list[ClosedIssue]` — recorded issue closures - `closed_prs: list[ClosedPR]` — recorded PR closures
**Read operations** implement filtering logic: - `list_issues()` filters by state, creator, and labels; respects limit - `get_issue_comments()` returns copy of stored comments list
---
### 4. Repository Resolution Utilities
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/repo_resolution.py`
#### Key Functions:
**`resolve_owner_repo(ctx: ErkContext, *, target_repo: str | None) -> tuple[str, str]`** - Resolves owner/repo from either `--repo flag` or local git context - If `target_repo` provided: validates "owner/repo" format (exactly one "/"), splits and returns - If `target_repo` None: extracts from `ctx.repo.github.owner` and `ctx.repo.github.repo` - Raises `UserFacingCliError` if format invalid or repo context unavailable
**`get_remote_github(ctx: ErkContext) -> RemoteGitHub`** - Gets or constructs RemoteGitHub instance - Priority: uses `ctx.remote_github` if available (for test injection), otherwise constructs `RealRemoteGitHub` from `ctx.http_client` and `ctx.time` - Raises `UserFacingCliError` if no http_client available
**`is_remote_mode(ctx: ErkContext, *, target_repo: str | None) -> bool`** - Determines if command should use remote path - Returns `True` if `target_repo` explicitly provided OR if `ctx.repo` is a `NoRepoSentinel` (no local git) - Returns `False` if local repo context exists and no explicit target
**`repo_option`** - Click option decorator for `--repo` flag - Type: str, default None - Help: "Target repo (owner/repo) for remote operation without local git clone"
---
### Summary
The RemoteGitHub gateway pattern is a clean separation of concerns:
1. **ABC** (`RemoteGitHub`) — 17 abstract methods covering auth, branches, commits, PRs, issues, and workflows 2. **Real** (`RealRemoteGitHub`) — HttpClient-based implementation with intelligent polling and pagination 3. **Fake** (`FakeRemoteGitHub`) — In-memory test double with constructor injection and mutation tracking 4. **Utilities** (`repo_resolution.py`) — CLI helpers for --repo flag handling, RemoteGitHub construction, and mode determination
This enables remote GitHub operations without a local git clone, used primarily by PR read commands that operate on external repositories.
